speaker: Alice
content: The peaceful facade of Wisteria Lane had recently been shattered, first by my suicide, and then by the discovery of a note among my belongings that suggested a suspicious reason for my desperate act.
mark: facade n. 假象，伪装;（建筑物的）正面，立面
shatter v. 破碎，碎裂；破坏；使极为惊愕难过 n. 碎片；受到极大打击的状态
of a 在美式口语中是高频连读组合，核心发音会弱读成 /əvə/
suggest v. 暗示，暗指；提议，建议；表明
suspicious adj. 感觉可疑的，怀疑的；不信任的suspect v. 猜想，认为
a reason for... 是常见搭配，…的原因
implication n. 可能的影响（或作用、结果）；含意，暗指；牵连，涉及

speaker: Alice
content: My friends gathered to discuss its implications.

speaker: Susan
content: I think we should give it to Paul.

speaker: Gabrielle
content: He's still mourning, Susan. He'll probably freak out.
mark: mourn v. 悼念，哀悼；对……感到痛心
freak out 使崩溃；极度不安 freak v.吃惊，不安，恼怒；

speaker: Susan
content: It doesn't matter. She was his wife. He deserves to have all the facts.
mark: It doesn't matter. 没关系

speaker: Bree
content: Well, we could do it gently. We could tell him about it over coffee and pastry.
mark: gently adv. 文静地，温柔地；轻柔地；渐进地，平缓地；
pastry n. 油酥面团；酥皮糕点

speaker: Lynette
content: That would be fun. "Paul, we have proof your wife killed herself over some deep, dark secret. Another bear claw?"
mark: proof n. 证明，证据；检验，证实 不可数名词
over some deep, dark secret. over prep.由于，关于  / over coffee and pastry. over prep. 在……期间  over for brunc
bear claw 熊爪：一种填充面点 claw n.（动物的）爪，爪子；

speaker: Gabrielle
content: We could always call the police.

speaker: Lynette
content: Maybe it's just some sort of sick joke.
mark: some sort of 某种类型的 其后通常接单数可数名词
sick joke 残酷的玩笑

speaker: Bree
content: Well, if it was a joke, it was in very poor taste.
mark: if it was a joke 是条件状语从句，表示假设的情况
poor taste 品味差 in poor taste 不得体：令人不悦或愤怒的方式

speaker: Susan
content: No, this was serious. I know it was. We got to find out what was going on.

speaker: Lynette
content: Let's say we do, there's a chance we're not going to like what we find.
mark: Let's say we do. 假设我们这样做。这句话通常用于提出一个假设或推测，然后探讨在这种假设下可能发生的情况或结果。
chance n. 可能性； 机会，时机；危险，冒险；运气，偶然
there's a chance that ...  存在…… 的可能性
There's a chance we're not going to like what we find. 我们有可能不会喜欢我们所发现的。

speaker: Susan
content: Well, isn't it worse to be in the dark? I mean, imagine she did all these horrible things.
mark: isn't it worse to be in the dark? 处在不知情的状态下难道不是更糟糕吗？
to be in the dark 在黑暗中，还常引申为蒙在鼓里；不知情

speaker: Bree
content: It's the age-old question. isn't it? How much do we really want to know about our neighbors?
mark: age-old 古老的；由来已久的

speaker: Alice
content: My friends were right to be concerned, they knew that every family has its secrets.
mark: My friends were right to be concerned. 朋友们的担心是对的。It's right to be nervous. 紧张是正常的
be right to 正确的，合适的，恰当的
concern v.使担心；有关，关于；涉及 adj. 担心的；相关的；关注的；关心的

speaker: Alice
content: And as my son and husband could have told them, you need to think carefully before digging them up.
mark: could have told 本可以告诉（实际没说）
dig up 挖掘；发现（秘密）

speaker: Alice
content: After talking for hours, my friends still hadn't agreed on what to do with the note.
mark: talk for hours 聊了好几个小时
agree on what to do with sth 就如何处理某事达成一致

speaker: Alice
content: So they decided to talk about it in the morning after a good night's rest. But no one could fall asleep that night.
mark: a good night's rest 好好睡一觉
all asleep 入睡 asleep adj.睡着的；麻木的，发麻的；

speaker: Alice
content: They each kept thinking of my suicide and how terribly alone I must have felt.
mark: alone adj. 独自的（强调状态），做形容词的时候只能做表语，不能做前置定语
lonely adj. 孤独的（强调情绪）

speaker: Alice
content: You see, loneliness was something my friends understood all too well.
mark: loneliness n. 孤独，寂寞
understand all too well 太明白了
